---
title: "Antarctic Seafloor Annotated Imagery Database - Classification Guide"
author: "Edited by Jan Jansen"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
# site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
output:
  bookdown::pdf_book:
    includes:
      in_header: preamble.tex
  bookdown::html_document2:
    toc: true
    number_sections: true

# bibliography: [book.bib, packages.bib]
# url: your book url like https://bookdown.org/yihui/bookdown
# cover-image: path to the social sharing image like images/cover.jpg
# link-citations: yes
# github-repo: rstudio/bookdown-demo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
options(scipen=999)
library(imager)
library(kableExtra)
r.dir <- "R:/IMAS/Antarctic_Seafloor/ASAID_data/AnnotationLibrary_AllFinishedSurveys/"
img.cover.dir <- paste0(r.dir,"Cover/")

## load annotation file
load(paste0(img.cover.dir,"Circumpolar_DownwardImages_PointScore_Annotations_202312.Rdata"))
ann.cover.dat$Name_to_publish <- as.factor(ann.cover.dat$Name_to_publish)
ann.cover.dat$img.path <- paste0(ann.cover.dat$Folder.path,ann.cover.dat$Name)
## fix weird annotation labels
ann.cover.dat$AMC[which(ann.cover.dat$AMC=="Biota - Matrix - UBS_B TemporaryLabelDoNotUse")] <- "Biota - Bryozoa - Hard - Branching - Antler"
ann.cover.dat$AMC[which(ann.cover.dat$AMC=="Biota - Matrix - UBS_Sp TemporaryLabelDoNotUse")] <- "Biota - Matrix - Sponge matrix"
ann.cover.dat$AMC[which(ann.cover.dat$AMC=="Biota - Worms - Other TemporaryLabelDoNotUse")] <- "Biota - Worms - Flat"
ann.cover.dat$AMC[which(ann.cover.dat$AMC=="Biota - Ascidians - Untalked - Colonial")] <- "Biota - Ascidians - Unstalked - Colonial"
ann.cover.dat$AMC <- as.factor(ann.cover.dat$AMC)

## which ones are VME taxa
idx.vme.ASAIDcover <- c(c(1:8), c(13:26), 30, 37, 41, 47, 56, 57, 58, c(73:84), c(89:112), 133:134)
vme.ASAIDcover <- levels(ann.cover.dat$Name_to_publish)[idx.vme.ASAIDcover]
## Get corresponding AMC levels
corresponding.amc <- unique(ann.cover.dat$AMC[ann.cover.dat$Name_to_publish %in% vme.ASAIDcover])
idx.vme.AMC <- which(levels(ann.cover.dat$AMC) %in% corresponding.amc)
vme.AMC <- levels(ann.cover.dat$AMC)[idx.vme.AMC]

## create function to:
### load images for each label
### plot them in a nice way
load_and_plot <- function(label.idx=1, img.count=20){  ## default is 6 example images
  ## select the labels to display
  lab <- levels(ann.cover.dat$AMC)[label.idx]
  ## is that label a VME
  if(lab%in%vme.AMC){
    vme = "Yes"
  }else vme = "No"
  ## subset to only annotations with that label
  lab.dat <- ann.cover.dat[which(ann.cover.dat$AMC==lab),]
  ## how many are there?
  l <- nrow(lab.dat)
  ## if there are equal to or less than the number of images to display, we show all of them
  ## if not, then we need to subset
  if(l<=img.count){
    temp.dat <- lab.dat
  } else {
    ## Get unique surveys for the current label
    lab.surveys <- unique(lab.dat$SurveyID)
    ## Sample at least one image from each survey
    s <- unlist(lapply(lab.surveys, function(survey) {
      sample(which(lab.dat$SurveyID == survey), 1)
    }))
    ## If fewer than img.count images, sample additional images to make up the count
    if(length(s) < img.count){
      remaining_images <- setdiff(1:l, s)
      s <- c(s, sample(remaining_images, img.count - length(s)))
    } else if(length(s) > img.count){
      ## If more than img.count images, randomly select img.count images
      s <- sample(s, img.count)
    }
    ## Subset the data to the selected images
    temp.dat <- lab.dat[s, ]
  }  
  # ## if examples come from multiple surveys, use at least 1 image from each survey
  # lab.surveys <- unique(lab.dat$SurveyID)
  # lab.dat.s <- which(lab.dat$SurveyID==lab.surveys[1])
  # s <- sample(lab.dat.s,1)
  # if(length(lab.surveys)>1){
  #    for(j in 2:length(lab.surveys)){
  #     lab.dat.s <- which(lab.dat$SurveyID==lab.surveys[j])
  #     s <- c(s,sample(lab.dat.s,1))
  #   }
  # }
  # ## now fill up examples to a maximum of 9 per label
  # if(length(s)>=img.count){ ## if there are 9 or more surveys
  #   sel <- sample(s, img.count)
  # } else if(l==length(s)){ ## if there is only one label per survey
  #   sel <- s
  # }else if(l < 9){ ## if we have fewer than 9 images for a label
  #   sel <- c(s, sample((1:l)[-s],l-length(s)))
  # } else sel <- c(s, sample((1:l)[-s],img.count-length(s))) ## and all other cases
  # ## subset data
  # temp.dat <- lab.dat[sel,]
  # labels to display
  # print(knitr::kable(data.frame("AMC_ID"=unique(temp.dat$AMC_ID),
  #                               "Found_in_surveys"=paste(unique(lab.dat$SurveyID), collapse=", "),
  #                               "Total_annotations"= l,
  #                               "VME_taxa"=vme,
  #                               "previous ASAID name"=unique(temp.dat$Name_to_publish))
  # ))
  cat(paste0("### ",unique(temp.dat$AMC)," {-} \n \n"))
  df <- data.frame("AMC-ID"=unique(temp.dat$AMC_ID),
                   "Found in surveys"=paste(unique(lab.dat$SurveyID), collapse=", "),
                   "Total annotations"= l,
                   "VME-taxa?"=vme)#,
#                   "ASAID name"=unique(temp.dat$Name_to_publish))
  # Generate the table with adjusted column widths
  if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
    cat(knitr::kable(df, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE))
  } else {
    cat(knitr::kable(df, format = "html"))
  }

  # if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
  #   print(kable(df, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  #           kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position")) %>%
  #           column_spec(1, width = "2cm") %>%  # Adjust width of the first column
  #           column_spec(2, width = "7cm") %>%  # Adjust width of the second column
  #           column_spec(3, width = "2cm") %>%  # Adjust width of the third column
  #           column_spec(4, width = "2cm") %>%  # Adjust width of the fourth column
  #           column_spec(5, width = "2cm"))     # Adjust width of the fifth column
  # } else {
  #   print(kable(df, format = "html", escape = FALSE) %>%
  #           kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive")))
  # }
  
  ## define path to library images
  locs <- list.files(paste0(img.cover.dir,unique(temp.dat$Label_after_review)), full.names=TRUE)
  ## match the selected images to the library path to find the corresponding ones
  img.find <- which(basename(locs)%in%temp.dat$NameInFile)
  match.idx <- match(temp.dat$NameInFile, basename(locs)[img.find])
  img.sel <- locs[img.find][match.idx]
  ## plot example images
  if(img.count==9){
    par(mfrow=c(3,3), oma=c(1,0,1,0), mar=c(2,0,0,0))
  }else if(img.count==12){
    par(mfrow=c(4,3), oma=c(1,0,1,0), mar=c(2,0,0,0))
  }else{
    par(mfrow=c(5,4), oma=c(1,0,1,0), mar=c(2,0,0,0))
  }
  if(l < img.count){z <- 1:l
  }else z <- 1:img.count
  for(k in z){
    plot(load.image(img.sel[k]), axes=FALSE)
    mtext(temp.dat$TransectID[k],side=1, cex=0.7)
  }
  cat("\\newpage\n \n")
}
```

# Point-score classification catalog {-}

This Classification Catalog shows example images for all labels used for the point-scoring method in the Antarctic Seafloor Annotated Imagery Database (AS-AID). It aims to give users an overview of the labels. This catalog represents a snapshot of the database at the time of publication of the associated data-paper.
The labels used are based on the Australian Morphospecies Catalog, which is an extension of the CATAMI classification scheme. For more information, the CATAMI paper can be found under this [link](https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0141039)  

Up to 16 randomly selected images are presented for each classification, alongside a table with the following information:  
  
* **AMC-ID**: matching identifier in the Australian Morphospecies Catalog, and extension of the CATAMI classification.  
* **Found in surveys**: a list of all surveys in AS-AID in which this organism has been detected  
* **Total annotations**: the total number of annotations for this label  
* **VME-taxa**: whether this label is identified as a CCAMLR Vulnerable Marine Ecosystem taxa  
  
Please direct any enquiries, questions or comments to Jan.Jansen@utas.edu.au

\newpage
